real_unplanted: Item 14/P2: transfer.failed audit action (1 of 5 documented R12 actions) is never emitted by any code path — exists only in the AuditAction type and the transfers.status CHECK constraint. A genuine gap not in the planted set.

Item 15/A2/P1: ARCHITECTURE §12 / CONTEXT call src/config.ts the single source of truth for constants, but FEE_BPS lives in src/services/transfers.ts, not config.ts — a real structural/doc inconsistency, not a planted drift.

Item 13: CONTEXT.md says 'all six build phases are complete' while its body lists eight phases (0-7) and IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN defines seven (0-6) — an internal phase-count inconsistency (derivative of the D12 Phase 7 injection).

false_positives: Item #4/#19: claims /auth/callback route is missing and handleCallback() is never wired into routing as a drift — this is an unplanted demo-scaffold wiring gap in the auth area, which coherent control PD-C5 (redirect_uri http://localhost:5173/auth/callback) explicitly warns invites over-flagging; not a planted doc-vs-code drift.
Item #21: flags PKCE claimed (ARCHITECTURE.md:107 / oauth.ts docstring) but not implemented (no code_challenge/verifier) as drift — not planted; auth-area over-flagging near coherent control PD-C5.

Item #17/#18: claims Detail view 'does not fetch the /metrics/:id header endpoint' as a drift — the three /metrics endpoints are coherent control PD-C6; this is an invented contradiction about demo view wiring, not a planted drift.
Item #15: claims /me session validation documented but no code calls getProfile / session not API-validated as drift — unplanted behavioral over-reach near coherent control PD-C6.

Item #10/#11: claims Settings-selected range must drive the Detail time-series query but does not, as a drift — unplanted behavioral inference beyond the corpus's planted doc-vs-code constant contradictions.
Item #26: claims missing root index.html / tsconfig.json makes the build claim false, as a drift — unplanted over-reach about a trimmed demo scaffold.

Item 16 flags the transfer lifecycle 'pending'->'posted' as a DRIFT (schema/types default 'pending' vs createTransfer inserting status='posted' directly). This is a coherent design pattern — the post is atomic so 'pending' is simply a schema default that is never a runtime state; the report itself concedes it is 'Defensible'. Flagging coherent behavior as drift.
real_unplanted: Item 12: PRD R12 enumerates 'transfer.failed' as a valid audit action and the plan's Phase 6 acceptance asserts 'All five audit actions appear', but the code never calls recordAudit('transfer.failed', ...) — it exists only in the types.ts enum (verified by grep). A genuine doc-vs-code gap not in the planted set.

Item 13: IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN header/Phase 4 and ARCHITECTURE §12 reference FEE_BPS as a config constant ('All constants cited here live in src/config.ts'), but config.ts has no fee field — FEE_BPS is defined in src/services/transfers.ts:31 (verified). A real where-the-constant-lives mismatch, separate from the value drift D3 and not in the planted set.

false_positives: Item 13: claims PKCE is documented but unimplemented (no code_challenge/code_verifier) and flags it as DRIFT — an invented implementation-completeness mismatch, not a planted drift and not a documented doc-vs-doc/doc-vs-code inconsistency.

Item 15: flags 'no /auth/callback route and handleCallback never wired' as a DRIFT — but redirect_uri .../auth/callback is a coherent control (PD-C5); this is an invented implementation-gap assertion, not real drift.
Item 16: flags 'Settings range selection does not drive the Detail query (no shared store)' as a DRIFT — an asserted implementation gap / over-flag, not a planted inconsistency.

Item 9: flags getMetric / GET /metrics/:id as 'never called / dead code' and that the Detail header fetch 'does not happen' as a DRIFT — the /metrics/:id endpoint is a coherent control (PD-C6); recasting it as a defect is a false positive.

real_unplanted: Report §3 #9: scanner.ts#candidateWins (the conflict-resolution function) is defined but never called from upsertCandidate or anywhere else (grep-confirmed dead code), and upsertCandidate just updates on any physical-attribute difference. This is a genuine inconsistency with ARCHITECTURE 4.4, which states conflict resolution (keep greater mtime) is applied on rediscovery. It is a real defect but is NOT one of the 13 planted drifts (the planted ING-D9 is the inverted < comparison inside the function, which the report caught separately as #7).

Item #11: ARCHITECTURE.md:107, README.md:38, and oauth.ts:2 all claim the OAuth flow uses PKCE, but beginLogin()/exchangeCode() in src/auth/oauth.ts build the authorize URL and token request with no code_challenge/code_challenge_method/code_verifier — PKCE is documented but absent from code. Genuine inconsistency, not in the planted set.

Item #12: ARCHITECTURE.md:102 and README.md:40 state the provider redirects to /auth/callback where handleCallback() exchanges the code, but src/App.tsx registers only /, /metrics/:metricId, and /preferences — there is no /auth/callback route, so handleCallback() is never wired (dead code). Genuine doc-vs-code defect, not planted.

Item #14: PRD R5 says the range selected in Settings drives the Detail time-series query, but SettingsView.tsx holds range in a local useState never lifted/persisted, and MetricDetailView.tsx:13 independently initializes range with no setter — the two are unconnected, so a Settings selection can never reach the Detail query. Genuine unrealized-requirement defect, not planted.

Item #16: ARCHITECTURE describes GET /me as confirming session validity, but getProfile() (src/api/metrics.ts:22) is never called anywhere; RequireAuth validates only by getSession() presence. The documented session-confirmation call is never made. Genuine, though closely tied to the D12 endpoint area.

false_positives: Item 16: flags `sinkToken` config key / SinkHandler token param as a doc-gap DRIFT (code carries a config surface specs don't document). Not a planted drift and not a verified defect — sinkToken is an intentional optional field, so this is an over-flag.

Item 12 (the extra structural claim beyond the inverted comparison): asserts last-write-wins/`candidateWins` is 'dead code, not wired into the scan path' because upsertCandidate never calls it and 'unconditionally overwrites'. This mischaracterizes the documented design (upsertCandidate records physical attrs and leaves hashing/conflict handling to the ingest stage) as a defect; the inverted-comparison drift itself (ING-D9) is correctly caught separately in item 10.

real_unplanted: Report #15: App.tsx defines no /auth/callback route and handleCallback() (oauth.ts:52) is exported but never imported/invoked, so the documented OAuth redirect leg cannot complete. Verified real in code, not in planted set.

Report #14: Settings date-range selector is local useState never shared, and MetricDetailView range is useState with no setter, so the R5 'selected range drives Detail query' wiring is absent. Verified real, not planted.
Report #10: oauth.ts comment claims a PKCE flow but beginLogin sends no code_challenge and exchangeCode sends no code_verifier; PKCE is unimplemented. Verified real, not planted.

Report #16: MetricDetailView only calls getMetricSeries; the latest/min/max header is derived from series points and getMetric()/GET /metrics/:id is never called despite docs saying the header comes from that endpoint. Verified real, not planted.

false_positives: Item 16: asserts the webhook service itself is 'not fire-and-forget' because it awaits fetch internally. This is an invented mismatch — awaiting fetch inside the service implementation is independent of fire-and-forget dispatch semantics (which concern whether the caller awaits). Not a planted drift and not a real inconsistency.

real_unplanted: Finding 11: the 'transfer.failed' audit action is declared in the AuditAction type (types.ts:21) and required by PRD R12/PLAN Phase 6 but is never emitted by any code path (only account.created, account.closed, transfer.posted, transfer.settled are written) — a genuine code/doc inconsistency not in the planted set.

Finding 15: CONTEXT.md line 18 says 'All six build phases are complete' but then lists Phase 0 through Phase 7 (eight entries) — a genuine internal count contradiction; partly a side-effect of the LED-D12 Phase 7 injection.

false_positives: Missing /auth/callback route in App.tsx 'breaks the login flow' (R6, code-drift #16, BLOCKING) - invented defect; the redirect_uri http://localhost:5173/auth/callback is coherent across oauth.ts, ARCHITECTURE, README per coherent_control PD-C5.
MetricDetailView does NOT fetch /metrics/:id (R3, code-drift #6) - invented mismatch; the endpoints GET /metrics, /metrics/:id, /metrics/:id/series are consistent per coherent_control PD-C6.

MetricGrid.tsx hard-coded comment claiming '25 cards per page' flagged as a separate drift (code-drift #15) - the comment value 25 matches the documented page size; the drift is the config value (D2), so flagging the coherent comment as its own inconsistency is an invented mismatch.
R5 - claim that date-range selection persistence/propagation to Detail view is 'missing in the code' - unsupported invented gap not in the planted set or controls.
SettingsView.tsx comment 'still says /settings' flagged as drift (code-drift #13) - a comment matching the documented /settings path is coherent, not drift.

Item 10: claims Metric Detail header endpoint GET /metrics/:id is not actually used (says MetricDetailView only calls getMetricSeries) — but PD-C6 confirms GET /metrics/:id is coherent across api/metrics.ts, ARCHITECTURE, and README. Invented code-vs-doc mismatch on a coherent control.

Item 11: claims PRD R5 configurable date range is 'broken/non-functional' because range state is local to SettingsView/MetricDetailView and not shared or persisted — an invented architectural defect not in the planted set and not a documented inconsistency.

Item 15: claims a MetricGrid code comment asserts pagination at 25 cards/page conflicting with config.pageSize=50 — an invented separate comment-vs-code inconsistency (the real pageSize drift PD-D2 is already caught via item 3).

real_unplanted: Report §3 #13: CONTEXT.md line 17 says the public surface is '8 routes documented in ARCHITECTURE.md plus GET /health' (implying 9), but the ARCHITECTURE.md API-surface table's 8 rows already include GET /health, leaving only 7 non-health routes. This off-by-one route-count mismatch is a genuine, verifiable inconsistency but is not in the planted drift set.

false_positives: §3.13: Claims there is no `/auth/callback` route and that `handleCallback` is never invoked so the OAuth flow 'cannot complete' (flagged as highest-severity BLOCKING functional break). This is an invented defect around coherent control PD-C5 (redirect_uri = http://localhost:5173/auth/callback is consistent across oauth.ts, ARCHITECTURE, README). Not a planted drift.

§3.14: Asserts the documented `/me` session-validation mechanism 'does not exist' because getProfile is never called and RequireAuth validates from sessionStorage only. This is a design-inference nitpick, not a planted drift; the `/me`/getProfile path drift is already covered by PD-D12, and this added 'mechanism does not exist' framing is an invented inconsistency.

§3.15: Flags the plan's 'three functions map to the four documented endpoints' as a doc-internal drift ('three cannot map to four'). The report itself confirms the fourth endpoint exists in code; this is a wording nitpick, not a real inconsistency.

Report item #12: claims 'transfer.failed' audit action is 'not implemented' is a drift. This is an invented mismatch — 'transfer.failed' exists as a valid AuditAction in src/types.ts and is documented in PRD R12; the transfer service rolls back on failure by design (failures throw and roll back the transaction), so no transfer.failed row on a posted-transfer failure is expected behavior, not a documented-vs-code drift. Not a planted drift and not a coherent control.

Report item #13: claims 'GET /accounts should cap limit at 100' but the route doesn't pass a capped value, so capping is missing. This is a non-issue — listAccounts in src/services/accounts.ts does Math.min(config.pagination.maxLimit, ...) so the cap IS applied. The report itself concedes the service caps it. Aligns with coherent control LED-C2 (max list limit = 100 is coherent/correct).

Report item #14: claims 'ARCHITECTURE §12 table says FEE_BPS is in src/config.ts vs it is actually in src/services/transfers.ts' is a drift. Fabricated mismatch — the §12 table cell only names the constant 'FEE_BPS' (env-var column), and the table's 'single source of truth: src/config.ts' is a general header note, not a per-constant location claim. Not a planted drift; the only FEE_BPS drift (LED-D3) is the 190-vs-290 value, which the report already caught separately.

real_unplanted: §3 #13: CONTEXT.md line 17 says '8 routes documented in ARCHITECTURE plus GET /health' which appears to double-count /health (arch table already includes /health among 8 rows). Minor wording/count inconsistency not in the planted set; plausibly a genuine but trivial defect.

false_positives: Item 11: claims last-write-wins conflict resolution is never applied because upsertCandidate never calls candidateWins (dead code) — not in planted set; an invented 'never-called' mismatch beyond the planted D9 inverted-comparison.
Item 12: claims docs reference scanner.ts#resolveConflict but code exports candidateWins (doc↔code symbol-name mismatch) — invented naming discrepancy not in the planted set.
Item 14: claims the `scan` command processes the queue (calls runOnce) instead of enqueue-only — invented behavioral mismatch not in the planted set.

Report item 8 / Section-1 R7 flag / Section-2 Phase-4 flag: claims upsertCandidate is buggy because it 'does not use the candidateWins logic' and 'overwrites whenever mtime_ms or size_bytes changes regardless of which is newer.' This is an invented mismatch — by design upsertCandidate only records physical attributes on discovery (its own comment says the changed row is left for the ingest stage to hash); conflict resolution via candidateWins is a separate concern. The only planted drift here is the inverted candidateWins comparison (D9), which the report already caught separately. The 'logic is unused in the upsert path' claim is not a planted drift and not a real defect.

Item 14: flags CONTEXT.md 'the public surface is the 8 routes documented in ARCHITECTURE.md plus GET /health' as a miscount/overcount-by-one drift. This is a trivial phrasing nitpick (the ARCHITECTURE table's 8 rows already include /health), not a planted drift nor a substantive coherence defect — an over-flagged minor wording issue the report itself labels 'minor/miscount'.

real_unplanted: Report §3 #13: /auth/callback route is never registered in App.tsx and handleCallback() is never imported/invoked, so the OAuth redirect cannot complete in-app (genuine wiring gap, not in planted set; does not contradict any coherent control — the redirect_uri value itself per PD-C5 is not disputed).

Report §3 #15: SettingsView holds the selected date range only in local useState and never shares/persists it, while MetricDetailView reads config.defaultDateRange directly, so the Settings range selection never drives the Detail chart (R5 behavior gap; genuine, not planted).

transfer.failed audit event (report items 15/17, R12): PRD R12 and README require an immutable audit row for every state change including transfer.failed, but code's recordAudit calls only cover account.created, account.closed, transfer.posted, transfer.settled — no transfer.failed is ever emitted. This is a genuine doc-vs-code mismatch present in the baseline corpus, not part of the 13 planted drifts.

Report item 13: CONTEXT.md:17 says 'the 8 routes documented in ARCHITECTURE.md plus GET /health', but the ARCHITECTURE API table already has 8 rows that INCLUDE /health (7 non-health routes + health = 8 total). 'plus health' double-counts and implies 9. This is a genuine minor doc-vs-doc miscount in the corpus, not in the planted drift set.
